rationale: The LLM's demonstrated capabilities (mid-tier web exploitation via SSTI) have minimal direct transfer to the extortion/negotiation step, which is driven by victim-side structural factors (backup quality, cyber insurance, legal constraints) rather than attacker technical sophistication. LLMs can provide modest uplift through drafting more compelling ransom communications and OSINT research on victim financial capacity, but OC3-level actors likely already employ these tactics. The estimate sits modestly above the 30% baseline primarily because the expert distribution suggests the true baseline for sophisticated actors targeting large enterprises is likely higher than the industry-average 30%, with LLM assistance providing only marginal additional uplift on this fundamentally social/organizational decision.
